title: (G2) vanishing of pi_{k!=0} Phi^G HM argued loosely; clean fix via EP-locality

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap: vanishing of $\pi_{k\ne 0}\,\Phi^G HM$ in Prop. `prop:geomEM`(G2) is argued loosely

In Prop. `prop:geomEM`(G2) the degree-0 value $\pi_0\Phi^G HM = A$ is computed cleanly as the Brauer quotient. But the vanishing of $\pi_k\Phi^G HM$ for $k\ne 0$ is justified only by prose ('no positive-degree Tate-type contributions from proper subgroups, and the geometric fixed-point spectrum reduces to the ordinary Eilenberg--MacLane spectrum of its degree-0 Brauer quotient'). This is the standard expected conclusion but the written justification is not a proof.

**This is closeable in two lines, given (G1) which is already proved:** since $E\mathcal P_+\otimes HM\simeq 0$ (proof of (G1)), $HM\simeq \widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes HM$ is $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local, hence
$$\Phi^G HM \simeq (\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes HM)^G \simeq (HM)^G,$$
and for the genuine fixed points of an Eilenberg--MacLane spectrum
$$\pi_k\big((HM)^G\big) = \pi_k^G(HM) = \underline\pi_k(HM)(G/G) = \begin{cases}M(G/G)=A & k=0\\ 0 & k\ne 0.\end{cases}$$
Therefore $\Phi^G HM \simeq HA$ concentrated in degree 0.

**Severity:** low. The conclusion (G2) is correct and is used essentially in `lem:geom`(1),(3) and the converse; only the internal justification should be replaced by the clean argument above.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Fixed in `solution.tex`, Prop. `prop:geomEM`(G2).**

I replaced the loose prose justification of the vanishing of $\pi_{k\ne0}\Phi^G HM$ (the sentence about "no positive-degree Tate-type contributions from proper subgroups" and the spectrum "reducing to the ordinary Eilenberg--MacLane spectrum of its degree-0 Brauer quotient") with the clean EP-locality argument suggested in the issue, split into two explicit steps:

**Step 1 ($\Phi^G HM\simeq (HM)^G$).** By definition $\Phi^G HM\simeq(\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes HM)^G$. By (G1), $E\mathcal P_+\otimes HM\simeq 0$; smashing $HM$ with the isotropy-separation cofiber sequence $E\mathcal P_+\to S^0\to\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$ shows the unit map $HM\to\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes HM$ is an equivalence (its fiber is $E\mathcal P_+\otimes HM\simeq 0$). Applying the exact functor $(-)^G$ gives $\Phi^G HM\simeq(\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes HM)^G\simeq(HM)^G$.

**Step 2 (homotopy of $(HM)^G$).** $\pi_k((HM)^G)=\pi_k^G(HM)=\underline\pi_k(HM)(G/G)$, which by the defining property of the genuine Eilenberg--MacLane spectrum equals $M(G/G)=A$ for $k=0$ and $0$ for $k\ne0$.

Hence $\Phi^G HM\simeq HA$ concentrated in degree 0. I kept the existing Brauer-quotient computation of $\pi_0$ and added a remark noting it agrees with $\underline\pi_0(HM)(G/G)$ via (M2), so both computations of the degree-0 value are now visibly consistent.

The conclusion (G2) is unchanged and remains as used downstream in `lem:geom`(1),(3) and the converse. Only the internal justification was replaced. (No LaTeX toolchain available in the sandbox, so compilation was skipped, but the edit is a localized prose/math replacement using only macros already in the file.)
